content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES
COMMISSION
June 28,
1995
Robert W.
Pike
Assistant City Attorney
City of Santa Barbara
740 State Street, Suite 201
Post Office Box 1990
Santa Barbara,
California
93102-1990
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
A-95-175
Dear Mr.
Pike:
You have requested advice regarding the conflict—of—interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the “Act”)
1 as they
pertain to Santa Barbara City Councilxnember Tom Roberts.
QUESTION
If Councilmember Roberts may not participate in one element
of the city’s annual general fund budget because that element of
the budget may affeót his source of income,
may he participate in
deliberations and approval of the annual general fund budget after
that element has been approved and adopted?
CONCLUSION
Although Councilmember Roberts may not participate in the
decision regarding the Human Services budget,
he may participate
in the other budgetary decisions before the city council provided
the segmentation procedure discussed below is followed.
FACTS
Mr.
Tom Roberts is a member of the Santa Barbara City
Council,
which will commence deliberations with the mayor and will
finally approve the city’s annual budgets.
The city’s general
fund budget has been in excess of $50,000,000 for the past three
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations,
Sections
18000—18954.
L2J Sici
s
X1) Sccc C’


File No.
A—95—175
Page
2
years.
One element of that budget is the Human Services budget,
which has been consistently about $447,000 for the past three
years.
However, this budget is subject to discretionary decisions
made by the city council.
Councilmember Roberts is a paid officer/director of Easy
Lift, Inc.,
a non—profit organization which has received funding
from the City Human Services budget.
Mr. Roberts has received
income in excess of two hundred fifty dollars from Easy Lift,
Inc., within the last twelve months.
Therefore, he will not
participate in any discussion or approval of the Human Services
budget.
After the allocation for the Human Services budget is
decided, applications for funding from the Human Services budget
will be received and processed.
The city council will approve
final allocations of the Human Services budget.
The nànprofit
organization for which Mr. Roberts serves as an officer may submit
an application for Human Services funding.
ANALYSIS
Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making,
participating in,
or using his official position to influence a
governmental decision in which he knows or has reason to know he
has a financial interest.
An official has a financial interest in
a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will
have a material financial effect, distinguishable from its effect
on the public generally,
on the official or a member of his
immediate family,
or on:
(a)
Any business entity in which the public
official has a direct or indirect investment worth
one thousand dollars
($1,000)
or more.
(b)
Any real property in which the public
off icial has a direct or indirect interest worth
one thousand dollars
($1,000)
or more.
(c)
Any source of income, other than gifts
and other than loans by a commercial lending
institution in the regular course of business on
terms available to the public without regard to
official status, aggregating two hundred fifty
dollars
($250)
or more in value provided to,
received by or promised to the public official
within 12 months prior to the time when the
decision is made.
(d)
Any business entity in which the public
official is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee,
or holds any position of management.
(e)
Any donor of,
or any intermediary or
agent for a donor of,
a gift or gifts aggregating


File No.
A—95—175
Page
3
two hundred fifty dollars
($250)
or more in value
provided to, received by,
or promised to the public
V
official within 12 months prior to the time when
the decision is made.
Section 87103.
As a public official,
Councilmember Roberts may not make,
participate in making, or in any way attempt to influence a
decision in which he has a financial interest.
(Section 87100.)
Since Easy Lift,
Inc., is a nonprofit organization, it is not
a “business entity” as defined in Section 82005.
Thus,
Easy Lift,
Inc., is not a disqualifying financial interest for purposes of
Section 87103(d).
However,
Easy Lift,
Inc., is a source of income
to Mr. Roberts since he serves as a salaried director of the
organization.
Therefore,
under the Act,
he does have a financial
interest which may be affected by the budget allocation decision.
(Section 87103(c).)
V
You have indicated that Mr. Roberts will not participate in
the discussion or approval of the Human Services budget.
The city
council determines the amount of the Human Services budget as part
of the annual city budget.
At a later date, organizations apply
to the Human Services agency for a funding allocation.
2
You have
inquired as to whether Mr. Ráberts may partcipate in the other
elements of the city’s annual general fund budget.
Seqinentation
V
Although Mr. Roberts will not participate with respect to the
budgetary decision regarding Human Services, it may be possible to
proceed with the other budget decisions by segmenting the
decisions.
(Schectman Advice Letter,
No. A-87-159.)
We have
previously advised the following procedure, which may be followed
to permit Mr. Roberts’ participation in other budgetary decisions:
(1)
The decisions for which the councilmember has a disqualifying
financial interest should be segregated from the other
decisions.
(2)
The decisions from which the councilmember is disqualified
should be considered first,
and a final decision reached by
the city council without the councilmember participating in
any way.
2
The city council will approve the final allocations of the
Human Services budget.
Please note that if Easy Lift, Inc.,
applies for funding from the Human Services agency,
Easy Lift,
Inc.
would be considered directly involved in the decision and Mr.
Roberts may not participate.
(Regulation 18702.1(b).)


File No.
A—95—175
Page
4
(3)
Once a decision has been made for which the councilmember has
a disqualifying financial interest,
he may participate in the
deliberations regarding the other issues,
so long as those
deliberations do not result in a reopening or in any way
affect the decisions from which he was disqualified.
(Huffaker Advice Letter,
No. A-86-343.)
Therefore,
Mr. Roberts may participate in the budget
decisions for which he does not have a conflict of interest only
if the segmentation procedure is followed and the Human Services
budget is considered first and a final decision is reached as to
that aspect of the budget.
I trust this answers your question.
Sincerely,
Steven G.
Churchwell
General Counsel
By:
Ji].ilt t c er
Coi
5
el,
Legal Division
SGC:JS: ak
